title: NIS2 Management Body Accountability and Governance Evidence
exclusions: Does not interpret national laws beyond the evidence objects listed, does not certify that individual directors met a specific legal duty of care, does not replace statutory annual accounts or minutes attested by corporate counsel, and does not auto-file supervisory notifications.
evidence: Scheduled saved search `UC-22.2.20` with `evidence_id` column, `index=audit_evidence sourcetype=evidence:governance`, corroborating `index=_audit` scheduler entries, and dashboard panel export signed per `evidenceSigning`.

compliance.assurance_rationale: Art.20 requires management bodies to approve and oversee cybersecurity risk-management measures and undergo training. The UC joins governance evidence (training completion, policy approvals, risk acceptances) from LMS and ServiceNow GRC against the management roster. It proves who was trained and which policies were approved, but cannot assess whether the oversight was substantively adequate, hence partial.
compliance.controlObjective: Proves management and supervisory bodies approve cyber risk measures on cadence, complete role-mapped training, and leave an auditable mix of policy sign-off, LMS completion, and risk acceptance tied to people on the NIS2 management roster.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search `UC-22.2.20` over `nis2_governance_evidence.csv` joined to `index=training` (`lms:completion`/`lms:enrollment`, `person_id`, `completion_date`) and `index=itsm` (`snow:sn_grc_policy`/`snow:task`, `policy_name`, `approval_date`, `approved_by`); `_audit` proves schedule runs; `audit_evidence` retains exports keyed by `evidence_id`.

controlTest.positiveScenario: Fixture `sample-data/uc-22.2.20-fixture.json` seeds `nis2_governance_evidence.csv` with `evidence_type=policy_approval`, `policy_name=ISMS-Cyber-2025`, `date=2024-01-10`, and injects no matching `snow:sn_grc_policy` event within 800 days, so `indexed_approval_epoch` is null, `governance_flag=MISSING`, and `evidence_id` is non-null for ticketing.
controlTest.negativeScenario: Fixture seeds the same policy row but adds `index=itsm` `approval_date=2026-03-01` with `approved_by=U12345`, producing `days_since<270`, `governance_flag=CURRENT`, and the alert search (which filters non-CURRENT flags) returns zero rows.

dataSources: Learning evidence lands in `index=training` with `sourcetype=lms:completion` or `sourcetype=lms:enrollment` after ingest via HEC or the Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Office 365 (Splunkbase 4055) for Azure AD–connected LMS exports; key extracted fields include `person_id`, `course_code`, `course_name`, `completion_date`, `score`, `role`, and `department`. ServiceNow GRC approvals and tasks are collected with the Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow (Splunkbase 1928) into `index=itsm` as `sourcetype=snow:sn_grc_policy` or `sourcetype=snow:task`, exposing `policy_name`, `approval_status`, `approved_by`, `approval_date`, `review_cycle`, and `next_review_date`. Registry rows come from lookup `nis2_governance_evidence.csv` enriched by `nis2_management_roster.csv` (`board_member`, `last_training_date`).
app: Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Office 365 (Splunkbase 4055) for LMS-to-cloud identity correlation; Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow (Splunkbase 1928) for `snow:sn_grc_policy` and `snow:task` pulls; Splunk Enterprise Security (Splunkbase 263) optional for correlation dashboards.
spl:
| inputlookup nis2_governance_evidence.csv
| lookup nis2_management_roster.csv person_id OUTPUT board_member, name, role, last_training_date
| eval roster_training_epoch=strptime(last_training_date, "%Y-%m-%d")
| join type=left person_id [
    search index=training sourcetype IN ("lms:completion", "lms:enrollment") earliest=-800d
    | eval completion_epoch=strptime(completion_date, "%Y-%m-%d")
    | stats max(completion_epoch) as indexed_training_epoch by person_id
]
| join type=left policy_name [
    search index=itsm sourcetype IN ("snow:sn_grc_policy", "snow:task") earliest=-800d
    | eval approval_epoch=strptime(approval_date, "%Y-%m-%d")
    | stats max(approval_epoch) as indexed_approval_epoch values(approved_by) as approval_chain by policy_name
]
| eval evidence_epoch=coalesce(indexed_training_epoch, indexed_approval_epoch, roster_training_epoch, strptime(date, "%Y-%m-%d"))
| eval days_since=if(isnotnull(evidence_epoch), round((now()-evidence_epoch)/86400, 0), null())
| eval governance_flag=case(
    isnull(evidence_epoch), "MISSING",
    days_since>365, "OVERDUE",
    days_since>270, "DUE_SOON",
    1=1, "CURRENT")
| eval evidence_id=sha256(evidence_type . "|" . coalesce(person_id, policy_name, responsible_person) . "|" . if(isnotnull(evidence_epoch), tostring(evidence_epoch), "null"))
| where governance_flag IN ("MISSING", "OVERDUE", "DUE_SOON")
| table evidence_id, evidence_type, board_member, name, role, person_id, policy_name, course_code, responsible_person, approval_chain, days_since, governance_flag, indexed_training_epoch, indexed_approval_epoch
| sort - days_since
description: Loads each expected governance obligation from `nis2_governance_evidence.csv`, enriches it with roster attributes, then left-joins the latest LMS completion epoch per `person_id` and the latest ServiceNow approval epoch per `policy_name`. It computes `days_since` from the newest proof across Splunk, the roster, or the static registry date, classifies rows as MISSING, OVERDUE (>365 days), or DUE_SOON (>270 days), hashes an `evidence_id`, and returns only rows that still need management attention.
value: Regulators and plaintiff theories under NIS2 focus on whether directors and officers actually exercised oversight—not only whether a policy PDF exists. This gives the board secretary and CISO a single place to prove who was trained, which cyber policies were approved by whom, and where evidence gaps exceed a one-year tolerance before audits or insurance renewals.
implementation: (1) Publish `nis2_governance_evidence.csv` rows for each required artefact (executive_training, policy_approval, risk_acceptance, board_security_briefing) with `person_id` or `policy_name`, baseline `date`, and `responsible_person`. (2) Keep `nis2_management_roster.csv` aligned with HR for `board_member` and `last_training_date`. (3) Ingest LMS completions into `index=training` with `sourcetype=lms:completion` and ServiceNow GRC into `index=itsm` using TA 1928. (4) Schedule the saved search weekly and alert when any `governance_flag` is OVERDUE or MISSING. (5) Archive the result set to `index=audit_evidence` and retain `index=_audit` action=log entries for the schedule.
visualization: Row 1 — Single value: count of OVERDUE plus MISSING rows. Row 2 — Table sorted by `days_since` with columns `governance_flag`, `name`, `role`, `evidence_type`, `policy_name`, `course_code`, `evidence_id`. Row 3 — Bar chart: counts by `evidence_type` and flag. Row 4 — Timeline: `approval_date` and `completion_date` overlays for drilldown.

references.title: Splunk documentation — Use the HTTP Event Collector

knownFalsePositives: 1) **ServiceNow change freeze — GRC tasks paused** — `approval_date` stops updating during platform freeze so Splunk shows OVERDUE. Distinguish: `index=itsm sourcetype=snow:task` `approval_status=In Progress` with `CHG*` bridge tickets. Suppress: time-bound exception in `nis2_exception_register.csv` keyed on `policy_name` with `until_epoch`. 2) **LMS completion delay after classroom board session** — directors finished training but the LMS vendor batch is 48 hours behind. Distinguish: roster `last_training_date` is fresh but `indexed_training_epoch` is null. Suppress: lookup `nis2_management_roster.csv` for `last_training_date` and extend `coalesce` to prefer that epoch until `index=training` catches up; add a temporary row to `nis2_exception_register.csv`. 3) **Azure AD UPN change — person_id mismatch** — new `person_id` breaks the join even though it is the same human. Distinguish: HR case `HR-` reference in ServiceNow with old and new IDs. Suppress: maintain `nis2_person_alias.csv` and `| lookup nis2_person_alias.csv obsolete_id OUTPUT new_person_id | eval person_id=coalesce(new_person_id, person_id)` upstream of the join. 4) **Policy superseded — old policy_name rows linger in registry CSV** — SN uses a new `policy_name` string and the join finds no approvals. Distinguish: `index=itsm` shows `policy_name=ISMS v3` while CSV still lists `ISMS v2`. Suppress: update `nis2_governance_evidence.csv` `policy_name` or add alias table `nis2_policy_alias.csv` with a `where` filter excluding retired names. 5) **Non-board executives appear MISSING for board-only course codes** — roster includes senior leaders who are not `board_member=yes`. Distinguish: `board_member` field from `nis2_management_roster.csv`. Suppress: filter `| where evidence_type!="executive_training" OR board_member="yes"`.
equipmentModels: Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow (Splunkbase 1928, GRC risk register and policy data)
Splunk Enterprise / Splunk Cloud (REST API for NIS2 programme health monitoring)
Splunk Enterprise Security 7.x (Splunkbase 263, notable event and incident metrics)
detailedImplementation:
## Prerequisites

• **Splunk**: Enterprise ≥9.2 or Splunk Cloud (current). Scheduling requires a search head not under memory pressure (check `index=_internal sourcetype=splunkd component=ProcessRunner` for OOM warnings before deploying a new scheduled search).
• **Technology Add-on**: Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow (Splunkbase app ID 1928) must be installed on search heads and indexers. Verify with `| rest /services/apps/local | search label="Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow" | table label version`.
• **Indexes**: `training`, `itsm` must exist on all indexers with retention matching the regulation's minimum (PCI: 1y, HIPAA: 6y, NIST 800-53: 1-3y per FIPS 199, SOX: 7y). Set `frozenTimePeriodInSecs` accordingly in `indexes.conf`.
• **Lookups**: `nis2_management_roster.csv`, `nis2_governance_evidence.csv` must be deployed to the search-tier app and refreshed on a documented schedule. For KV Store lookups, verify with `| rest /servicesNS/-/-/data/transforms/lookups | search name IN ('nis2_management_roster.csv','nis2_governance_evidence.csv')`.
• **Search-head capacity**: Subsearch-based detections need `subsearch_maxtime` ≥ 60s and `subsearch_maxout` ≥ 50000 in `limits.conf`. For high-volume environments, tune `[search] max_searches_per_cpu` up from default.
• **Regulatory mapping**: This detection evidences clause `Art.20`. The control objective is: "Proves management and supervisory bodies approve cyber risk measures on cadence, complete role-mapped training, and leave an auditable mix of policy sign-off, LMS completion, and risk acceptance tied to people on the NIS2 management roster." — every alert payload should include this text so downstream consumers know the compliance purpose.

## Step 1 — Configure data collection

Data collection is performed by the Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow (Splunkbase app ID 1928), landing events in `training`, `itsm` under `lms:completion`, `lms:enrollment`, `snow:sn_grc_policy`, `snow:task`. REST-polled ticket tables produce 500-5000 records per sync per table; at 300s interval expect ~3-8 GB/month for a mid-size service desk.

Configure the input through Settings > Data Inputs > ServiceNow Service Account. A reference `inputs.conf` stanza looks like:

```ini
[snow://{instance_slug}]
url = https://<instance>.service-now.com
table = {table_name}
interval = 300
index = itsm
sourcetype = {sourcetype}
```

The following fields are authoritative for this detection once the TA parses them: `number, state, priority, assignment_group, opened_at, closed_at, sys_updated_on, caller_id, category, subcategory`. Verify they are extracted at search time with `index=training earliest=-15m | fieldsummary` before scheduling the detection.


5 operational considerations apply to the rollout of this detection:


• **Hint 1:** Publish `nis2_governance_evidence.csv` rows for each required artefact (executive_training, policy_approval, risk_acceptance, board_security_briefing) with `person_id` or `policy_name`, baseline `date`, and `responsible_person`. The register referenced here is primary evidence — treat changes to it with the same discipline as code changes.

• **Hint 2:** Keep `nis2_management_roster.csv` aligned with HR for `board_member` and `last_training_date`. This is an operational consideration that influences either the detection's accuracy or its maintainability — document the approach chosen in the UC runbook.

• **Hint 3:** Ingest LMS completions into `index=training` with `sourcetype=lms:completion` and ServiceNow GRC into `index=itsm` using TA 1928. Ingestion SLA drift is usually invisible until it breaks something — monitor the feed's freshness with a companion search that alerts when the newest event is older than the expected cadence.

• **Hint 4:** Schedule the saved search weekly and alert when any `governance_flag` is OVERDUE or MISSING. Schedule cadence is a tradeoff between regulatory latency and search-head load; document the chosen cadence and the reasoning in the UC runbook.

• **Hint 5:** Archive the result set to `index=audit_evidence` and retain `index=_audit` action=log entries for the schedule. Schedule cadence is a tradeoff between regulatory latency and search-head load; document the chosen cadence and the reasoning in the UC runbook.

## Step 2 — Implement the search

For "NIS2 Management Body Accountability and Governance Evidence," the aggregation is the evidence artifact itself — auditors will read these rows as the official record of NIS2 control activity.

This search correlates `index=training` (sourcetype=`lms:completion`) on the outer side with `index=itsm` (sourcetype=`lms:enrollment`) on the inner side, joined on `person_id` across the last 800 days. `type=left` preserves all rows from the outer search; nulls in the inner side indicate missing data that may itself constitute a finding.

Join caveat: Splunk's `| join` subsearch is capped at `max_searches_per_cpu * subsearch_maxout` results (default: 50000). If the inner subsearch exceeds this, rows are silently dropped and the resulting report will under-count. Check `index=_internal sourcetype=splunkd log_level=WARN subsearch` for truncation warnings. For inner searches expected to return >10000 rows, rewrite as `| stats` with a larger base search and post-filter, or use `| tstats` if a CIM datamodel covers the data.

Time-zone sensitivity: the `strptime(last_training_date, "%Y-%m-%d")` conversion assumes the source timestamp is in the Splunk server's local time zone unless the format includes `%z`. If `last_training_date` is emitted in UTC by the source system but Splunk's search head runs in a non-UTC zone, the resulting epoch will be off by the zone offset and the subsequent `where _time > last_training_date_epoch` comparison will produce systematic false positives or negatives. Always validate by comparing `strftime(last_training_date_epoch, "%Y-%m-%d %H:%M %Z")` against a known-good reference event before promoting to production.

**Schedule configuration** (`savedsearches.conf`):
```ini
[UC-22.2.20: NIS2 Management Body Accountability and Governance Evidence]
cron_schedule = 0 */6 * * *
dispatch.earliest_time = -800d
dispatch.latest_time = now
actions = summary_index
action.summary_index._name = summary_compliance
action.summary_index.marker = uc=22.2.20,reg=NIS2
```

## Step 3 — Validate deployment

**(a) Field extraction check:** Run the following to confirm the TA is extracting every field the detection relies on:

```spl
index=training sourcetype=lms:completion earliest=-1h
| fieldsummary
| where count=0
| table field
```

Any row returned is a field the TA claims to extract but isn't appearing in recent data — it's either a rename upstream, a TA version mismatch, or a data-format change.

**(b) Required-field presence:** This detection specifically needs: `approval_chain`, `board_member`, `course_code`, `days_since`, `evidence_id`, `evidence_type`, `governance_flag`, `indexed_approval_epoch`, `indexed_training_epoch`, `name`. Run:

```spl
index=training sourcetype=lms:completion earliest=-1h
| head 100
| table approval_chain, board_member, course_code, days_since, evidence_id, evidence_type, governance_flag, indexed_approval_epoch
```

Every row should have non-null values for the fields the detection's `where` clause filters on. Nulls cause silent drops in the result set.

**(c) Lookup freshness:** The detection joins against `nis2_management_roster.csv`. Check its modification time and row count:

```spl
| inputlookup nis2_management_roster.csv
| eval _lookup_row=1
| stats count as rows
```

Compare the row count to the expected baseline; an unexpected drop is a sign the upstream export truncated.

**(d) Threshold calibration:** Before enabling as a production alert, run the base search with the threshold removed and profile the distribution of `days_since`:

```spl
<base search> earliest=-30d
| stats perc50(days_since) as p50 perc90(days_since) as p90 perc99(days_since) as p99 max(days_since) as hi
```

The current threshold `365` should sit between p90 and p99 for a balanced signal-to-noise ratio. If it's below p50, expect heavy alert volume; if above p99, expect under-detection.

**(e) Negative test:** Generate a synthetic 'should not alert' event using the Splunk Sample Generator or a crafted `| makeresults` event and confirm the detection does NOT fire on it. A detection that fires on everything is equivalent to a detection that fires on nothing.

## Step 4 — Operationalize (dashboard and reporting)

Create a companion dashboard titled `UC-22.2.20: NIS2 evidence` with the following panel layout:

**Panel 1 — Row 1 — Single Value: Count Of Overdue Plus Missing Rows. Row 2 — Table Sorted By `Days Since` With Columns `Governance Flag` (the key fields):** Configure according to standard Splunk visualization patterns for this panel type.

**Panel 2 — `Name` (the key fields):** Configure according to standard Splunk visualization patterns for this panel type.

**Panel 3 — `Role` (the key fields):** Configure according to standard Splunk visualization patterns for this panel type.

**Panel 4 — `Evidence Type` (the key fields):** Configure according to standard Splunk visualization patterns for this panel type.

**Panel 5 — `Policy Name` (the key fields):** Configure according to standard Splunk visualization patterns for this panel type.

**Panel 6 — `Course Code` (the key fields):** Configure according to standard Splunk visualization patterns for this panel type.

**Panel 7 — `Evidence Id`. Row 3 — Bar Chart: Counts By `Evidence Type` And Flag. Row 4 — Timeline: `Approval Date` And `Completion Date` Overlays For Drilldown (the key fields):** Configure according to standard Splunk visualization patterns for this panel type.


Time picker defaults: set to last 30 days with named presets for 24h, 7d, 30d, 90d, and 1y — the auditor needs all five windows without retyping. Enable scheduled PDF export for the full dashboard on the regulation's reporting cadence.

## Step 5 — Troubleshooting and regulation-specific caveats

**No results when violations exist:** The most likely cause is subsearch truncation (see Step 2). Inspect `index=_internal sourcetype=splunkd component=searchprocessor log_level=WARN subsearch` for messages like 'Output result count exceeded 50000.'

**Search times out:** Check the search job inspector (`Activity > Jobs > <this UC's last run> > Inspect`). The phase consuming the time (fetch, command, summary) indicates whether the issue is data-layer (too wide a window, unaccelerated datamodel), command-layer (expensive `transaction` or `eventstats`), or post-aggregation.


**NIS2-specific failure modes:**

• 24/7 reporting obligation (Art. 23): NIS2 requires early warning within 24 hours of awareness of a significant incident. Detections that back this SLA must compute elapsed time from the SOC's first-acknowledged timestamp (not the resolve-time) and must trigger independently of business hours — the regulatory clock does not pause overnight.

• Member-state variation: NIS2 is transposed individually by each EU Member State with some latitude on thresholds and sector scoping. A detection correct for German IT-SiG 3 (BSI) may be misaligned with Dutch CWV or French LPM. Use `nis2_member_state_config.csv` to parametrize thresholds per jurisdiction; never hardcode.

• Essential vs Important entity criteria: the applicable obligations depend on sector + size. Before alerting, verify the monitored entity's designation; a detection firing on an out-of-scope subsidiary causes unnecessary regulatory notifications that erode trust with the national CSIRT.

• Supply-chain (Art. 21(2)(d)) extent: NIS2 explicitly includes direct suppliers' cybersecurity posture. Detections must cover monitored-by-you-not-owned-by-you infrastructure (outsourced SaaS, managed SIEM tenants) — these are in scope even if operationally run by a third party. BA/BAA-style governance applies.
grandmaExplanation: We keep a checklist of what our leaders must sign off on and which security courses they have finished, and we raise a clear flag when anything on that list is missing or older than a year.
